folio.label: 41v
folio.l: In hosen rede , he went ful fetislye
I clad he was , ful smal and ¸prelye
Al in a kirtil , of a ligút vaget´
Ful fair & thikke , ben the poyntis set
And ther vp onö , he had a gay surplice
As white as is , the blosme on the rice
A mery child he was , so god me saue
Wel coude he laten blod , & clippe and shaue
And make a charter of lond , or acquietance
In xx é maners , coude he tryppe and daunce
After the scole , of Oxenford tho
And w hise leggis , casten to and fro
And pleyen song , on a smal ribible
Ther to he song som tyme , a loude quynyble
And as wel coude he pley , on a geterne
In al the Tounö nas there , brewhous ne Tauäne
That he ne vesited , with his solace
There ony gaylard , tapstere was
But sothe to seyn , he was somdele squeymous
Of fartyng , and of speche daungerous
This Absolouö , that ioly was and gay
Goth with a Censer , on the haliday
Sensyng the wyues , of the Parissú fast
And many a louely loke , on hem he cast
And namely , on this Carpenteres wyf
To loke on hire , hi~ æougút a mery lif
She was so ¸pre , and swete , and likerous
I dar wel seyn , if she had ben a Mous
And he a kat , he wold hire hent a nonö
This parissú Clerk , this ioly Absolouö
Hatú in his hert , swicú a loue longyng
That of no wyf , toke he nonö offryng
For curteisie he seide , he wold nonö
The Mone , whan it was nygút , ful brigút shonö
And Absolouö , his gytterne , hath I take
For ¹amoås he æougút , for to wake
And forth he goth , Iolyf and amerous
Til he cam , to the Carpenters hous
A litel aftä Cokkes , had I crowe
And dressed hi~ vp , by a shot wyndowe
That was vp on , the Carpenteres wal
He syngeth in his voys , gentil and smal
Now dere lady , if thy wyl be
I pray ©ow , æat ©e wol , rewe on me
Ful wel accordyng , to his gitternyng
